summary: Hotz proposes a crypto-based scheme to 'vampire attack' Twitter by cloning it on a blockchain and incentivizing both content creators and consumers to migrate through token rewards. The plan involves scraping Twitter via browser extensions, rewarding original posters with cryptocurrency, and creating a clean ad-free interface to lure users away. He argues this approach solves the chicken-and-egg problem of platform migration by paying both sides to switch. The post dismisses doubters and claims the technical challenges are solvable through TLS verification and leveraging Twitter's existing anti-Sybil measures.
key_points: Platform migration fails because you need creators and users to move simultaneously - a vampire attack solves this by financially incentivizing both sides
Browser extensions could scrape Twitter by rewarding users with tokens for providing unseen tweets to the blockchain
Creators would migrate for 'free money' while lurkers would switch for an ad-free experience without fake trending topics
Technical objections about data authenticity and Sybil attacks can be addressed through TLS verification and bootstrapping off Twitter's existing systems
The real barrier isn't technical feasibility but people's unwillingness to attempt ambitious projects
money_quotes: Here's a simple way to take over Twitter for way less than $43 billion dollars.
The creators on Twitter are the liquidity providers, the lurkers are the users.
Tons of creators would migrate for the FREE MONEY!
Even lurkers will use TWITCHAIN front end because it doesn't have ads and fake 'trending' topics.
Watch the 'liquidity' flow quickly from Twitter to TWITCHAIN.

The real truth of HN doubt is that anything is possible, because then they'd have to look inside and ask themselves the hard question about why they didn't do it and instead just make a comfortable salary at FAANG while claiming nothing can ever change.
I would absolutely love to see this. I'm not a big scale person, and you have to go big with this and hit strong all at once.
key_insight: Platform lock-in can be broken by using cryptocurrency incentives to simultaneously pay both content creators and consumers to migrate, solving the coordination problem that kills most Twitter alternatives.
